description:
# Update every expected.clif / expected.specs across fixtures

## Goal

Walk the `fixtures/` tree. For every fixture, regenerate the
golden files against the post-reducer output and review each diff.
The diff should be **reviewable as 'this output is reasonable for
this source'** — the agent-review checkpoint the design promises
in lieu of a numeric predictor.

## Per-fixture review checklist

For each fixture:
- Runtime output (`expected.txt`) UNCHANGED.
- `expected.clif` reflects the reduced IR. Diff should be:
  - Fewer specs (often by 10x+ for pattern-matching fixtures).
  - No vestigial cont specs from singleton-input cascade.
  - main may collapse to constants for fully-static programs.
- `expected.specs` reflects the smaller spec set.

## Fixtures expected to change dramatically

`polymorphic`, `higher_order`, `closure_typed_captures`,
`curried_add`, `ast_eval`, `mutual_recursion`, `fib_tailrec`,
`list_primitives`, `spawn2_basic`, `spawn_with_captures`.

## Fixtures expected to change minimally

`tail_recursion` (count_100k — budget caps unrolling),
`concurrency_ping_pong` (receive boundary stays),
`relay` (receive boundary stays).

## Acceptance

- `cargo test` green.
- All three paths (interp + JIT + AOT) produce identical output
  for every fixture.
- ast_eval CLIF specifically drops from ~1400 lines to under 100
  (the headline metric).

## Removals

None.
title: fz-RED.6: re-bless every fixture golden
